Algeria.text: Algeria squeezed through the group stage on the back of a breathless 3-3 draw with Austria — a match that twice seemed to be settling in their favour before Kalajdzic's 96th-minute equaliser snatched the point back, leaving them to advance as one of the best third-placed sides rather than outright winners of Group J. Switzerland await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a genuinely even contest between two teams of comparable continental pedigree — neither side commands obvious favouritism, and Algeria's attacking threat, underlined by Mahrez's brace, gives them every reason for confidence. Survive that, and Colombia or Ghana could follow in the last 16, with Argentina lurking as a potential quarterfinal shadow over any deep run.

Argentina.text: Argentina swept through Group J with something close to authority, beating Jordan 3-1 in a match they controlled from the outset — two goals before the half-hour mark settled the contest, and though Jordan pulled one back midway through the second period, Messi's late finish restored the margin and the composure. Cape Verde await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, a fixture the world champions enter as heavy favourites; the Islanders are capable of disrupting rhythm, but Argentina's quality in the final third makes this a routine assignment on paper. Survive that, and the bracket opens towards potentially Brazil, England, or Mexico in the semifinal — the kind of opposition where tournaments are truly decided.

Australia.text: Australia ground out a goalless draw with Paraguay to close the group stage — functional rather than inspiring, a result that secured second place without ever threatening to announce themselves as a serious force. Egypt await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT, a tie that looks manageable on paper but carries genuine bite: the Africans are no soft touch, and the Socceroos' blunt attack offers little margin for error in a contest that figures to be tight and attritional. Survive that, and the prospect of Argentina in the Round of 16 looms as a defining test of just how far this squad can go.

Austria.text: Austria scraped through Group J in the most nerve-shredding fashion: a 3-3 draw with Algeria that swung on a Mahrez brace either side of the ninetieth minute before Kalajdzic's equaliser deep in stoppage time spared them the ignominy of elimination. That is not the form of a side that inspires dread, and Spain — poised, well-organised, and among the genuine heavyweights of the draw — will carry considerable expectation into the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT. Survive that, and Croatia or Portugal lurk in the Round of 16, ensuring Austria's road to the quarterfinal runs through quality at every turn.

Belgium.text: Belgium required every minute of extra time to see off Senegal, trailing 2-0 after the hour before Lukaku pulled one back in the 86th minute, Tielemans equalised three minutes later, and then stepped up to convert a penalty deep into stoppage time — a recovery that spoke more of resilience than authority. The Red Devils now face the host nation USA in the Round of 16 on Mon Jul 6 17:00 PT, a tie that Belgium enter as the more seasoned side on paper but one the Americans, roared on by their home crowd, will make anything but comfortable. Should they advance, Spain or Portugal could await in the quarterfinal — a tie of a rather different order altogether.

Bosnia & Herzegovina.text: Bosnia & Herzegovina's tournament ended with a flat 0-2 defeat to hosts USA in the Round of 32, a result that flattered neither side's ambitions — the Dragons offered little going forward and were punished either side of a half-time goal from Balogun and a late Tillman strike that killed the tie. They had reached the knockout stage as one of the best third-placed teams from Group B, scrapping through on four points after a mixed group campaign, and that survival instinct ultimately ran dry against a USA side playing in front of its own crowd.

Brazil.text: Brazil edged past Japan in a nervy Round of 32 tie that looked comfortable until Sano's early goal put Seleção on the back foot — it took Casemiro to restore order midway through the second half before Martinelli's stoppage-time strike settled it with a late flourish that masked genuine Japanese resistance. Norway await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT, a stern but navigable assignment: Brazil's pedigree demands them as favourites, though Norway's direct, physical style will test a defence that has not always looked watertight. Survive that, and England or Mexico could stand between Brazil and the last four, with France, Spain, or Portugal potentially looming as final opponents.

Canada.text: Canada squeezed through their Round of 32 tie against South Africa on the slenderest of margins, Stephen Eustáquio's goal deep into stoppage time sparing them what would have been a painful early exit on home soil. The drama of that late rescue act sets the stakes for Saturday, when Morocco arrive for a Round of 16 confrontation at Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT — a genuine examination, with the Moroccan side carrying the weight of a nation that has come to expect deep tournament runs and the tactical discipline to make any opponent uncomfortable. Survive that, and a quarterfinal on Thu Jul 9 13:00 PT could bring France, whose pedigree demands respect at every stage. Progress further still, and the semifinal draw opens onto a landscape containing Spain and Portugal among others — the kind of opposition that would demand far more convincing performances than the one Canada just managed to scrape past.

Cape Verde.text: Cape Verde ground through Group H without conceding a goal, though three consecutive draws — culminating in a goalless stalemate against Saudi Arabia — tell the story of a side built on defensive discipline rather than flair. That pragmatic solidity now meets its sternest examination: Argentina await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 15:00 PT, a contest in which the Blue Sharks enter as heavy underdogs against one of the tournament's foremost contenders. Should the improbable occur, Australia or Egypt would follow in the Round of 16, with Colombia or Switzerland potentially lurking in the quarterfinals beyond that.

Colombia.text: Colombia closed out the group stage with a goalless draw against Portugal — a disciplined, organised performance that reflected a side content to bank top spot rather than chase a game they did not need to win. They enter the knockout rounds in good shape, unbeaten and with the best defensive record their group record suggests. Ghana await in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT, a tie Colombia approach as clear favourites, though West African sides have a habit of making these occasions uncomfortable for fancied South American opponents. Should they come through, Argentina loom as a possible quarterfinal opponent — a rivalry that would electrify the tournament.

Croatia.text: Croatia edged past Ghana in a nervy finish, Vlasic's 83rd-minute goal making safe what Sucic had opened up before the hour, Ghana pulling one back through Luckassen to ensure an uncomfortable closing stretch. Second place in Group L is the reward, and the Round of 32 brings a formidable assignment: Portugal, Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT — a side with the firepower to punish any defensive lapse, making Croatia underdogs despite their tournament pedigree. Survive that, and Austria or Spain await in the Round of 16, before a potential quarterfinal collision with Belgium or the host nation USA could define how far this generation's Croatian side can travel.

Curaçao.text: Curaçao's group stage ended with a 0-2 defeat to Ivory Coast, Nicolas Pépé doing the damage with goals either side of half-time — the first arriving inside seven minutes to set a tone the Curaçao defence never recovered from. They finished fourth in Group E with a single point and a goal difference of minus nine, yet still advanced to the Round of 32 as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides. There, their journey came to an abrupt end, the gulf in quality across the group stage ultimately a true reflection of where this Curaçao side stands on the world stage.

Czech Republic.text: Czech Republic's group stage ended with a thumping 3-0 defeat to Mexico, the goals arriving in a clinical second-half burst and a stoppage-time finish that underlined the gulf in class — a deflating exit for a side that managed just one point across three matches. Having finished fourth in Group A and crept through as one of the best third-placed teams, they were nonetheless unable to sustain that precarious foothold, and Mexico's composed, purposeful display exposed their limitations emphatically. The Czechs depart at the earliest opportunity, their tournament leaving little to build on.

DR Congo.text: DR Congo led England for the better part of an hour in the Round of 32, Cipenga's seventh-minute opener giving them every reason to believe before Harry Kane dismantled the dream with two clinical finishes in the final quarter-hour. It was a cruel, late unraveling — the Leopards had defended resolutely and threatened a genuine upset — only for England's captain to remind everyone why tournament football rewards patience and predatory quality. DR Congo exit at the last-32 stage, their campaign ultimately defined by the agony of a lead surrendered too late to recover.

Ecuador.text: Ecuador's exit arrived without much ceremony — a 0-2 defeat to Mexico in the Round of 32 in which they never truly threatened, conceding twice before half-time through Quiñones and a Jiménez strike deep in first-half stoppage time. The pattern was emphatic: Ecuador chasing the game from the half-hour mark, unable to find a response across 90 minutes. They had navigated the group stage only as one of the better third-placed sides, and against a Mexican outfit with genuine tournament ambition on home soil, that fragile foundation gave way entirely.

Egypt.text: Egypt's group stage closed with a frantic early exchange against Iran — Saber's fifth-minute opener answered almost immediately by Rezaeian, the game settling into a 1-1 draw that secured second place without much comfort. The Pharaohs face Australia in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 11:00 PT in what should, on paper, represent a manageable assignment, though the Socceroos are capable of disruption and Egypt's defensive wobbles invite pressure. Survive that, and Argentina could await in the Round of 16 — a test of an altogether different magnitude and the kind of occasion that defines tournament campaigns.

England.text: England needed a Harry Kane rescue act to see off DR Congo in the Round of 32 — Cipenga's seventh-minute opener left them rattled for the better part of an hour before Kane struck twice in the final quarter-hour to seal a nervy 2-1 win, the kind of performance that raises questions even in victory. Mexico await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 17:00 PT, a fixture that carries genuine edge: El Tri are no soft touch, and England's habit of conceding first will be tested again. Should they advance, Brazil or Norway loom in the quarterfinal, with the likes of France, Spain, or Portugal conceivable opponents should this run extend all the way to the final.

France.text: France dismantled Sweden 3-0 in the Round of 32, Mbappé bookending a Barcola goal either side of half-time to make it a controlled, professional evening with the tie effectively settled before the hour. Paraguay await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, and while the South Americans are no pushovers, Les Bleus enter as heavy favorites — this has the look of a routine assignment for a side that has yet to concede a knockout goal. Should France advance deep enough, Spain or Portugal could loom in the semifinal, a reminder that the real examination lies further down the road.

Germany.text: Germany's Round of 32 exit is as brutal as the format can produce: Havertz's second-half equaliser cancelled out Enciso's opener, and when neither side could be separated across 90 minutes, Paraguay held their nerve in the shootout, converting four of four while Germany stumbled to three. A group-stage winner undone before the last sixteen, Joachim Löw's side — or whoever currently stewards this generation — departs a tournament they entered with genuine ambitions, beaten not by a superior team across the pitch but by the cold lottery of penalties.

Ghana.text: Ghana finished their group stage with a defeat that carried more sting than the scoreline alone suggests — leading Croatia through Luckassen's equaliser on 73 minutes, they conceded a Vlasic winner ten minutes from time to exit Group L in third place. They advanced regardless as one of the best third-placed sides, and now face Colombia in the Round of 32 on Fri Jul 3 18:30 PT — a genuine test against a side with the individual quality and tactical structure to trouble any opponent at this stage. Survive that, and Algeria or Switzerland await in the last sixteen, with the tantalising but distant possibility of Argentina looming in the quarterfinal.

Haiti.text: Haiti depart the 2026 World Cup without a point to their name, their group stage exit confirmed by a 4-2 defeat to Morocco that was comfortable for the Atlas Lions despite a spirited Haitian showing either side of half-time. Two goals inside the first 43 minutes gave Haiti brief cause for optimism, but Morocco reasserted themselves emphatically in the closing stages, scoring four times in total to send their opponents home. The Caribbeans finish bottom of Group C, undone by a gulf in quality that proved too wide to bridge across three matches.

Iran.text: Iran's group-stage finale against Egypt ended 1-1, a Rezaeian equaliser on 14 minutes cancelling out Saber's early opener — a pattern that defined their entire tournament: always in the contest, never quite through it. Three draws, no wins, and three points were enough to sneak through as one of the best third-placed sides, but the knockout round delivered the reckoning that the group stage had deferred. Iran exit the 2026 World Cup without a victory to their name, their campaign a study in stubborn resilience that ultimately fell short of the cutting edge a deep run demands.

Iraq.text: Iraq's group stage ended in humiliation against Senegal, a 5-0 defeat that was effectively settled inside the opening minutes and then ruthlessly extended across the second half, with Gueye alone scoring twice in a 12-minute spell. Finishing fourth in Group I with three losses, no points, and twelve goals conceded, Iraq's tournament was over at the earliest stage — a chastening introduction to a World Cup where the gulf in class between the continent's elite and the tournament's newcomers was laid painfully bare.

Ivory Coast.text: Ivory Coast gave Norway a genuine contest in the Round of 32, pulling level through Diallo on 74 minutes after falling behind to Nusa's first-half opener — only for Haaland to settle it with four minutes remaining, a late hammer blow that extinguished the comeback. The Elephants depart a tournament they had navigated with enough promise to suggest they belonged, yet ultimately could not withstand Norway's cutting edge when it mattered most.

Japan.text: Japan held their own against Brazil for the better part of an hour, Sano's 29th-minute opener giving them genuine belief before Casemiro levelled midway through the second half. It was Martinelli's stoppage-time winner that delivered the cruellest of exits — a Samurai Blue side that had navigated Group F without defeat ultimately undone in the final seconds of a Round of 32 tie they had come agonisingly close to taking further.

Jordan.text: Jordan's group stage ended with a 3-1 defeat to Argentina — a result that followed the script almost from the first whistle, with the South Americans establishing a two-goal cushion inside half an hour before Messi put the contest beyond doubt late on. Al-Tamari's consolation just after the hour offered a brief flicker, but Jordan were outclassed. Finishing fourth in Group J with three defeats and no points, they nevertheless advanced as one of the tournament's best third-placed sides before their World Cup came to a close.

Mexico.text: Mexico dispatched Ecuador with something close to authority, a first-half double — Quiñones threading through before Jiménez added a second in first-half stoppage time — leaving the second period as a formality and confirming a flawless group stage. The reward is England in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 17:00 PT, a genuine heavyweight clash in which El Tri enter as underdogs against one of the tournament's most formidable sides, though their perfect record and defensive solidity make them anything but straightforward opposition. Survive that, and Brazil or Norway could await in the quarterfinal — another potential step up in class that would define whether this Mexican side is merely impressive or something more.

Morocco.text:
Morocco's Round of 32 against the Netherlands was settled in the most dramatic fashion — a stoppage-time equaliser from Diop cancelling out Gakpo's opener before the Atlas Lions held their nerve to win on penalties. The match was a genuine test, and the manner of their survival will have rattled a few brackets.

Canada await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 10:00 PT, a tie Morocco enter as clear favourites; the hosts have made their tournament history simply by qualifying and lack the pedigree to trouble a side this experienced in knockout football. Should Morocco advance, France or Paraguay loom in the quarterfinal on Thu Jul 9 13:00 PT — a potential collision with France representing by far the sterner reckoning — and a semifinal path that could yet place Spain or Portugal in their way.

Netherlands.text: Netherlands dominated their group with seven points and a healthy goal difference, only to suffer one of the tournament's crueller exits — Gakpo's 72nd-minute strike cancelled out by a Moroccan equaliser deep in stoppage time, before the Dutch lost the subsequent shootout 2-3. It was the worst kind of departure: a lead surrendered at the death, then the lottery of penalties delivering the final blow. A side that had looked like genuine contenders through the group stage goes home from the Round of 32, undone in the space of twenty minutes.

New Zealand.text: Belgium administered a thorough lesson in the final group game, with Trossard's brace and further goals from De Bruyne and Lukaku turning what might have been a contest into a rout — New Zealand's consolation arriving far too late, in the 84th minute, to suggest any real foothold. The All Whites finished fourth in Group G with a single point and a goals difference of minus ten, their tournament record a frank reflection of the gulf between them and the competition's established forces. They depart a World Cup that offered little comfort beyond the experience itself.

Norway.text: Norway edged past Ivory Coast in a Round of 32 tie that followed a familiar nerve-shredding script: Nusa's first-half opener looked to be holding until Diallo equalised with quarter of an hour left, only for Haaland to settle it with four minutes to go. Now comes the stiffest possible test — Brazil await in the Round of 16 on Sun Jul 5 13:00 PT, and for all Haaland's menace up front, Norway enter as clear underdogs against one of the tournament's heavyweight favourites. Survive that, and England or Mexico could stand between them and the last four, with the likes of Argentina and France lurking as potential opponents in the later rounds.

Panama.text: Panama's group stage exit was as comprehensive as the record suggests — three defeats, no goals scored, and a closing loss to England that was settled efficiently in a five-minute spell midway through the second half, Bellingham and Kane putting the game to bed without drama or late anxiety. They finish bottom of Group L with nothing to show from their three outings, and whatever hope they carried into the tournament evaporated long before the knockout rounds began. A familiar story for Central American football at this level: the gap between qualifying and competing proved too wide to bridge.

Paraguay.text: Paraguay squeezed through the Round of 32 in the most dramatic fashion, leading Germany through Enciso's first-half strike before Havertz equalised nine minutes after the break, and the Albirroja held their nerve to win 4-3 on penalties. That result — against one of world football's most storied programmes — announces them as something more than grateful qualifiers. France await in the Round of 16 on Sat Jul 4 14:00 PT, a formidable assignment against a squad stacked with elite talent, though Paraguay arrive with hard-won momentum and a proven penalty temperament. Survive that, and Canada or Morocco could stand in the quarterfinal way, with the prospect of Spain, Portugal, or Brazil lurking in the later rounds.

Portugal.text: Portugal ground out a goalless draw with Colombia to close their group campaign, a functional, uninspired finish that secured second place in Group K without ever threatening to ignite. They turn next to Croatia in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 16:00 PT — a tie that carries genuine weight on both sides, two technically accomplished sides with a history of meeting at tournaments, though Portugal's superior attacking resources make them narrow favorites. Survive that, and Spain or Austria await in the Round of 16, with the prospect of Belgium or the host nation United States looming in the quarterfinal beyond.

Qatar.text: Qatar's group stage exit arrived with little ceremony — a 1-3 defeat to Bosnia & Herzegovina in which they trailed by two goals before half-time, Al-Haydos pulling one back on the stroke of the interval before Mahmic's 80th-minute strike killed any lingering hope of a comeback. The hosts finished bottom of Group B with a single point and a goal difference of minus eight, their tournament a sobering exercise in the gap between ambition and execution at this level.

Saudi Arabia.text: Saudi Arabia's group stage ended with a goalless draw against Cape Verde — a stalemate that was as flat as the scoreline suggests, neither side able to manufacture a breakthrough across ninety minutes. That result, combined with a goal difference of minus five across three matches, proved just enough to squeeze through as one of the best third-placed teams, but the Green Falcons were eliminated in the Round of 32, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Scotland.text: Brazil ended Scotland's tournament before it had truly begun, a 3-0 defeat that was every bit as one-sided as the scoreline suggests. Júnior struck twice in the first half — the second arriving deep in added time — before Cunha added a third shortly after the interval, leaving Scotland with nothing to build on. They depart having advanced from the group stage as one of the best third-placed sides, a modest distinction that flatters a campaign yielding just one win and four goals conceded. The 2026 World Cup ends for Scotland at the Round of 32.

Senegal.text: Senegal led Belgium twice and carried that advantage deep into extra time, only for Romelu Lukaku to level in the 86th minute and Youri Tielemans to strike twice in three agonising minutes — the second a penalty in stoppage time of extra time — to seal a 3-2 defeat that was as cruel as knockout football gets. The Lions of Teranga had done enough to advance from Group I as one of the best third-placed teams, yet Belgium proved a step too far in the Round of 32. Their tournament ends there: a gutting exit for a side that gave everything and were ultimately undone by a three-minute collapse they could not have scripted in a nightmare.

South Africa.text: South Africa's World Cup ended in the cruelest fashion, a 90th-minute goal from Eustáquio giving Canada a 1-0 win that the balance of the match may not have fully demanded. Bafana Bafana had navigated the group stage with enough grit to finish second in Group A, but a tournament played on home soil — or at least in their confederate hemisphere — ultimately closed in the dying seconds of a Round of 32 tie they very nearly survived.

South Korea.text: South Korea close their World Cup 2026 account with a flat 0-1 defeat to South Africa, Maseko's goal just past the hour mark enough to settle a match the Taeguk Warriors never truly threatened to turn. They had squeezed through the group phase as one of the best third-placed sides, but that slender lifeline ran out in the round of 32 — eliminated without a goal scored in their final two outings, their tournament ending as quietly as it had begun.

Spain.text: Spain closed the group stage with a controlled, professional win over Uruguay — Baena's first-half strike the only goal required as the reigning European champions kept a third clean sheet and topped Group H without conceding once. Austria await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 12:00 PT, a fixture Spain will approach as heavy favorites; the Austrians are a tidy, well-organized side but a different class of problem to the one Luis de la Fuente's squad has so far sailed through. Should Spain advance as expected, Croatia or Portugal lurk in the Round of 16 — either a genuine examination of nerve — with Belgium or the United States a possible quarterfinal obstacle before the tournament's weightier business begins.

Sweden.text: Sweden's tournament ends with a 0-3 defeat to France in the Round of 32 — a verdict that was effectively delivered at half-time, Mbappé's first strike just before the break puncturing whatever hope the Swedes had carried into the tie, and a Barcola goal eight minutes into the second half rendering the contest settled long before his second. They had advanced from Group F as one of the better third-placed sides, a narrow path that always pointed toward exactly this kind of reckoning: a French side operating at a different altitude. The margin was honest.

Switzerland.text: Switzerland closed out the group stage with a composed 2-1 win over Canada, taking control through two early second-half goals before absorbing a late consolation — a professional finish that confirmed them as Group B winners. Algeria await in the Round of 32 on Thu Jul 2 20:00 PT, a winnable assignment for a Swiss side that has proved well-organized and difficult to break down, though Algeria carry enough quality to punish complacency. Survive that, and a quarterfinal collision with Argentina looms as a genuine possibility — the kind of occasion that has historically exposed Switzerland's ceiling at major tournaments.

Tunisia.text: Tunisia's group stage exit was written early: two Dutch goals inside seven minutes buried the Carthage Eagles before the match had found its shape, and though Mastouri pulled one back after the break, Hecke's strike shortly after the hour restored the three-goal cushion and sealed a 3-1 defeat. Tunisia finished bottom of Group F without a point, their record of three defeats and a goal difference of minus ten telling the story of a tournament in which they were consistently outclassed — a dispiriting campaign ended against one of the competition's heavyweights.

Turkey.text: Turkey's tournament ends in the cruelest of fashions — a 3-2 win over the United States that arrived too late to matter. They trailed inside three minutes, fought back to lead through Güler and Yilmaz, conceded again early in the second half, and only sealed it through Ayhan's injury-time strike. The drama was real, the effort undeniable, but a fourth-place group finish — even as one of the better third-placed sides — proved an insufficient foundation, and Turkey's World Cup 2026 is over at the group stage.

USA.text: USA dispatched Bosnia & Herzegovina with a clean 2-0 in the Round of 32, Balogun breaking the deadlock on the stroke of half-time before Tillman put the result beyond doubt eight minutes from the end — a controlled, professional performance from a side growing into the tournament. Belgium await in the Round of 16 on Mon Jul 6 17:00 PT, and for all the hosts' momentum, this is a genuine examination: the Belgians carry the quality and experience to punish any complacency, making it a proper test rather than a formality. Should the Americans come through, Portugal or Spain could be lurking in the quarterfinal, with France a possible obstacle in the last four.

Uruguay.text: Uruguay's group stage ended with a narrow 0-1 defeat to Spain, Álex Baena's first-half strike the solitary difference in a contest that offered the Uruguayans little joy in the final third. Despite advancing as one of the best third-placed sides, their tournament came to an abrupt halt at the same juncture — two draws and a loss across Group H, with a negative goal difference, telling a story of a side that never quite found its footing at this World Cup.

Uzbekistan.text: Uzbekistan's group stage ended with a 1-3 defeat to DR Congo, a result that traced a familiar arc: an encouraging early lead through Shomurodov, then a second-half capitulation as Wissa and Mayele dismantled them after the hour mark, Wissa adding a third in stoppage time to put a harsh gloss on the scoreline. The Central Asians finished bottom of Group K without a point, conceding eleven goals across three matches, and no late reprieve arrived. Their debut on the expanded World Cup stage was brief and bruising, a tournament that exposed the gulf between ambition and readiness at the highest level.
